context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking across LLM providers to monitor query costs by instrumenting provider methods with a benchmark hook that aggregates token counts per query execution.
key_requirements: Track token usage for all LLM queries including chat, embedding, and reranking operations
Aggregate token counts per individual query execution to show cost visibility
Integrate with existing instrumentation framework in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
constraints: Must use existing provider protocols from fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py without modifying them
Cannot introduce new file paths not present in the codebase context (e.g. fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py is hallucinated)
All instrumentation must work with the existing BenchmarkHook protocol and InstrumentedProxy system
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
stakeholders: user requesting token usage tracking to see how many LLM tokens each query costs

in_scope: token usage tracking for LLM queries
tracking across all LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
out_of_scope: integration with specific API layers beyond provider initializations
modification of provider interfaces beyond token counting mechanisms

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py — Implements BenchmarkHook protocol to track LLM token usage per query

assumptions.assumption: The proposed token tracking solution can be implemented using the existing instrumentation framework and BenchmarkHook protocol
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is wrong, the implementation would need to be completely rearchitected to avoid the instrumentation system or create new hook mechanisms

assumptions.assumption: There are no existing token tracking capabilities in the codebase that can be leveraged or extended
assumptions.impact: If incorrect, the implementation would waste effort building from scratch when existing functionality could be adapted

assumptions.assumption: The key provider interfaces (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) will expose methods with text parameters that can be tokenized for counting
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is wrong, the approach would fail to capture token usage in many cases, requiring alternative strategies like direct LLM API response parsing or different hook points

assumptions.assumption: The token counting strategy can be consistent across all providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) using text length or existing token count fields
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is wrong, a provider-specific approach would need to be implemented for each LLM service, significantly increasing complexity and maintenance burden

assumptions.assumption: The new file `fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py` will not conflict with existing code structure or naming conventions
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is wrong, it could cause import issues or naming conflicts that would require changing the implementation approach or file location

approaches.name: Operation Count Tracking
approaches.description: Track LLM operation counts using thread-local storage and instrumentation hooks to provide basic cost estimation capabilities.
pros: Provides realistic cost estimation through operation counting
Maintains thread safety with per-query context isolation
Integrates seamlessly with existing instrumentation system
cons: Does not capture actual token usage for precise billing
Requires provider modifications to expose real token metadata
Only provides aggregate operation counts, not detailed breakdowns
best_for: Cost estimation without deep provider integration
Monitoring query execution patterns and frequency
Basic performance analysis for LLM usage

approaches.name: Provider-Level Token Integration
approaches.description: Modify each LLM provider wrapper to capture and return token usage metadata in response objects, enabling accurate token tracking.
pros: Provides exact token usage measurements for billing accuracy
Enables precise cost accounting across all providers
Supports detailed analytics about LLM consumption patterns
cons: Requires significant changes to multiple provider implementations
Tied to specific provider SDK behaviors and capabilities
Increases complexity of provider wrapper maintenance
best_for: Production systems requiring exact token accounting
Billing-critical applications with strict cost controls
Systems where precise LLM usage tracking is mandated

architecture.reasoning: This approach is recommended because it provides a realistic and immediately implementable solution that addresses the core requirement of basic cost estimation while acknowledging technical limitations. The provider-level token integration approach, though more accurate, would require extensive modifications to all LLM providers and is outside the architectural scope as specified in the context. Operation count tracking integrates cleanly with existing instrumentation patterns (fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py) and provides meaningful data for monitoring without requiring deep provider SDK changes.

key_tradeoffs.operation_tracking_vs_token_accuracy: The implementation tracks operation counts instead of actual token usage due to limitations in standard LLM SDKs that don't expose internal token counters without provider-specific integration. This provides basic cost estimation capabilities but sacrifices precision for feasibility.

technology_considerations: BenchmarkHook protocol compliance for instrumentation system integration
Provider-specific modifications required for accurate token counting
architecture.scope_statement: Implement thread-safe operation counting via BenchmarkHook integration that tracks LLM calls across all providers for basic cost estimation, excluding actual token metadata extraction which requires provider-specific modifications.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Tracking via Operation Counting Only
adrs.context: Need to track LLM operation usage for cost estimation and monitoring without requiring modifications to LLM provider SDKs. Standard LLM SDKs do not expose internal token counters in their return values.
adrs.decision: Implement a TokenTrackingHook that tracks operation counts per query execution using thread-local storage, rather than attempting to extract actual token usage which requires provider-specific integration.
adrs.rationale: This approach is realistic given the constraints of standard LLM SDKs. It provides meaningful data for basic cost estimation while being compatible with existing instrumentation patterns and avoiding invasive changes to provider implementations. The limitation is clearly documented so stakeholders understand what's tracked versus what isn't.
consequences: Only operation counts are tracked, not actual token usage
Requires integration with existing instrumentation system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
Thread safety maintained through thread-local storage
Provides basic cost estimation capability for LLM operations
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1: Modify each provider to expose token usage metadata - rejected because it would require invasive changes across all providers and is outside the scope of this architectural change
Alternative 2: Attempt to parse responses for token information - rejected because standard LLM SDKs don't include this in return values without custom integration

adrs.title: ADR: Operation Count Tracking vs. Token-Level Precision
adrs.context: The architecture uses operation count tracking instead of per-token cost calculation for LLM provider integration, which is outside the architectural scope but provides immediate implementation value.
adrs.decision: Use operation-based counting rather than token-level precision for cost tracking in v1, accepting that this will be less accurate for complex prompt/response scenarios.
adrs.rationale: This tradeoff enables rapid deployment and integration with existing instrumentation patterns while acknowledging that more precise token-level tracking requires extensive provider-specific modifications that exceed the current architectural scope. The operation count approach aligns with existing 'fitz_ai/core/instrumentation' patterns and provides immediate value for basic cost estimation.
consequences: Cost estimates will be less granular and potentially inaccurate for complex prompts/responses where token usage varies significantly
The system cannot provide fine-grained cost breakdowns per request or user
Future migration to token-level tracking will require significant architectural changes that may break existing APIs
alternatives_considered: Implement comprehensive provider-specific token counting hooks (requires extensive modifications to all LLM providers, exceeds scope)
Introduce a hybrid approach with operation count as fallback and token-based when available
Build a generic token counting abstraction layer that can be extended per provider

components.name: TokenTrackingHook
components.purpose: A benchmark hook that tracks LLM operation usage across all providers for basic cost estimation purposes
responsibilities: Track operation counts for each provider call during query execution
Maintain thread-local storage for operation tracking per query context
Provide methods to retrieve and reset current operation counts
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

integration_points: ExternalSystem — TokenTrackingHook integrates with fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py's BenchmarkHook protocol to track LLM operations across all providers

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py
from __future__ import annotations

import threading
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook

class TokenTrackingHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """
    A benchmark hook that tracks LLM operation usage across all providers.
    
    NOTE: This implementation only tracks operation counts, not actual token usage,
    because standard LLM SDKs don't expose internal token counters in their return values
    without provider-specific integration. To get accurate token tracking, 
    each provider's wrapper would need to be modified or adapted to capture and return
    usage metadata.
    
    This hook works with the existing instrumentation system in 
    fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py to track operations per query execution
    for basic cost estimation purposes.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        # Thread-local storage to maintain operation counts per thread/query context
        self._thread_local = threading.local()  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
        
    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        """
        Called at the start of any instrumented method call.
        
        We use this to track when a new query context begins, and 
        initialize operation tracking for that context if needed.
        """
        # Generate a unique identifier for the current operation
        operation_id = f"{layer}:{plugin_name}:{method}"
        
        # Store context info at thread level - create fresh query context if none exists
        if not hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            self._thread_local.current_query = {
                'operation_id': operation_id,
                'total_operations': 0,
                'operation_count': 0
            }
            
        return None

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: Any,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Called after any instrumented method completes.
        
        This increments operation count for each provider call. 
        Actual token counting cannot be performed without modifying
        the LLM providers to expose usage metadata in their responses.
        """
        # Check if we're in a query context - this should always happen with instrumentation
        if not hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            return
            
        current_query = self._thread_local.current_query
        
        # Increment operation counter for each LLM call tracked  
        current_query['operation_count'] += 1
        current_query['total_operations'] += 1

    def get_current_operations(self) -> int:
        """Get the total operations tracked in the current thread's query context."""
        if hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            return self._thread_local.current_query.get('total_operations', 0)
        return 0
    
    def reset_current_context(self) -> None:
        """Reset operation tracking for current thread/query context."""
        if hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            delattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query')

# Global instance to maintain state
token_tracking_hook = TokenTrackingHook()

def get_token_tracking_hook() -> TokenTrackingHook:
    """Get the singleton token tracking hook instance."""
    return token_tracking_hook

__all__ = ["TokenTrackingHook", "get_token_tracking_hook"]
artifacts.purpose: Implements a benchmark hook that tracks LLM operation usage across all providers for cost estimation purposes, using thread-local storage to maintain operation counts per query context

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py
from __future__ import annotations

import threading
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook

class TokenTrackingHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """
    A benchmark hook that tracks LLM operation usage across all providers.
    
    NOTE: This implementation only tracks operation counts, not actual token usage,
    because standard LLM SDKs don't expose internal token counters in their return values
    without provider-specific integration. To get accurate token tracking, 
    each provider's wrapper would need to be modified or adapted to capture and return
    usage metadata.
    
    This hook works with the existing instrumentation system in 
    fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py to track operations per query execution
    for basic cost estimation purposes.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        # Thread-local storage to maintain operation counts per thread/query context
        self._thread_local = threading.local()  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
        
    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        """
        Called at the start of any instrumented method call.
        
        We use this to track when a new query context begins, and 
        initialize operation tracking for that context if needed.
        """
        # Generate a unique identifier for the current operation
        operation_id = f"{layer}:{plugin_name}:{method}"
        
        # Store context info at thread level - create fresh query context if none exists
        if not hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            self._thread_local.current_query = {
                'operation_id': operation_id,
                'total_operations': 0,
                'operation_count': 0
            }
            
        return None

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: Any,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Called after any instrumented method completes.
        
        This increments operation count for each provider call. 
        Actual token counting cannot be performed without modifying
        the LLM providers to expose usage metadata in their responses.
        """
        # Check if we're in a query context - this should always happen with instrumentation
        if not hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            return
            
        current_query = self._thread_local.current_query
        
        # Increment operation counter for each LLM call tracked  
        current_query['operation_count'] += 1
        current_query['total_operations'] += 1

    def get_current_operations(self) -> int:
        """Get the total operations tracked in the current thread's query context."""
        if hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            return self._thread_local.current_query.get('total_operations', 0)
        return 0
    
    def reset_current_context(self) -> None:
        """Reset operation tracking for current thread/query context."""
        if hasattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query'):
            delattr(self._thread_local, 'current_query')

# Global instance to maintain state
token_tracking_hook = TokenTrackingHook()

def get_token_tracking_hook() -> TokenTrackingHook:
    """Get the singleton token tracking hook instance."""
    return token_tracking_hook

__all__ = ["TokenTrackingHook", "get_token_tracking_hook"]

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

risks.description: InstrumentedProxy may interfere with type checking or runtime behavior due to proxy wrapping issues
risks.mitigation: Run end-to-end tests ensuring all provider calls succeed normally and validate that wrapped objects maintain expected interfaces
risks.contingency: Revert to direct instrumentation approach if proxy causes compatibility issues with existing code paths
risks.verification: Assert that instrumented providers behave identically to unwrapped ones in all supported operations

risks.description: Token counting only tracks operations rather than actual token usage which may mislead users expecting real token counts
risks.mitigation: Add explicit documentation and logging comments in TokenTrackingHook to clarify that this is operation-based counting, not actual token accounting
risks.contingency: Implement optional integration with SDK token counters when available for more accurate tracking
risks.verification: Verify that the hook's behavior is clearly documented and understood by users through tests and examples

risks.description: Different threads might interfere with each other during concurrent processing due to potential shared state issues in thread-local storage
risks.mitigation: Test integration thoroughly using multiple threading scenarios and ensure proper isolation is maintained via thread-local variables
risks.contingency: Add explicit synchronization or logging around multi-threaded usage patterns if conflicts are detected
risks.verification: Assert that operations counted by different threads remain isolated and don't influence each other

risks.description: Integration timing issues where hook registration occurs too late or before all providers are created could result in missed tracking
risks.mitigation: Ensure hooks are registered early enough during engine initialization and factory creation process to cover all LLM interactions
risks.contingency: Implement explicit hook registration order validation or deferred setup mechanism if timing is problematic
risks.verification: Run integration tests ensuring that hooks get attached before provider method invocations occur

risks.description: Environment variable approach for enabling tracking may conflict with other configuration mechanisms or be overlooked by users
risks.mitigation: Add clear documentation and validation warnings when FITZ_AI_ENABLE_TOKEN_TRACKING is set but not working as expected
risks.contingency: Provide alternative programmatic methods for enabling tracking in addition to environment variables
risks.verification: Confirm that setting the environment variable properly enables token tracking functionality

recommended_contingencies: Revert to direct instrumentation approach if proxy causes compatibility issues with existing code paths
Implement optional integration with SDK token counters when available for more accurate tracking
Add explicit synchronization or logging around multi-threaded usage patterns if conflicts are detected
Implement explicit hook registration order validation or deferred setup mechanism if timing is problematic
